impact: ATP production is critically low. Persistent fatigue, physical and cognitive impairment are likely.
actions: Urgent clinician review for underlying mitochondrial or thyroid disorder
B-vitamin complex (B1, B2, B3, B5) daily
expected_outcome: Measurable improvement in Energy score toward 50+ within 8–12 weeks with consistent protocol.

impact: Cellular energy production is below threshold, reducing stamina and slowing physical recovery.
actions: Post-meal walk 10–15 min after main meals
expected_outcome: Energy score should improve toward 55–60 within 6–8 weeks with consistent adherence.

impact: Energy pathways are functioning but not at full capacity, limiting peak performance.
actions: CoQ10 100 mg/day if not already taking
Increase complex carbohydrate timing around activity
expected_outcome: Energy score stable at 60+ achievable within 4–6 weeks with lifestyle adjustments.

impact: Inflammatory signalling is critically elevated. Ongoing tissue damage and accelerated recovery impairment likely.
actions: Urgent clinician review for autoimmune or chronic inflammatory condition
Eliminate inflammatory dietary triggers (refined sugar, seed oils, processed foods)
Stress management protocol: daily relaxation practice
expected_outcome: Inflammation score reduction toward <65 expected within 8–12 weeks with strict protocol.

impact: Persistent inflammatory signalling is increasing tissue stress and delaying recovery.
actions: Reduce omega-6 seed oil intake (sunflower, soy)
expected_outcome: Inflammation score should reduce toward 55–60 within 6–8 weeks.

impact: Low-grade inflammation may be silently increasing recovery time and energy consumption.
expected_outcome: Bringing Inflammation to below 55 achievable within 4–6 weeks with diet changes.

impact: Detox pathways are severely impaired. Toxin accumulation, oxidative overload and systemic burden are highly likely.
actions: Urgent clinician review for hepatic or detox pathway assessment
Eliminate alcohol and hepatotoxic substances immediately
Cruciferous vegetables daily (broccoli, kale, Brussel sprouts)
expected_outcome: Detox score improvement toward 45+ expected within 8–12 weeks with strict protocol.

impact: Detox efficiency is below threshold, increasing the risk of sluggish metabolic clearance and oxidative stress.
actions: Reduce alcohol and environmental toxin load
Review medications for hepatic load with clinician
expected_outcome: Detox score should reach 55+ within 6–8 weeks with consistent adherence.

impact: Detox capacity is functional but not optimal, slightly increasing systemic toxin clearance time.
symptoms: Slow recovery from alcohol or illness
expected_outcome: Detox score reaching 65 achievable within 4–6 weeks with lifestyle corrections.

impact: Neurotransmission and cognitive resilience pathways are critically reduced. Persistent brain fog, mood disruption and stress dysregulation expected.

Sleep hygiene protocol (fixed wake time, dark room)
Daily stress management (20 min meditation or breathwork)
expected_outcome: Brain score improvement toward 45+ expected within 8–12 weeks with consistent protocol.

impact: Key neurotransmitter synthesis and signalling pathways are under-performing, affecting cognitive function and mood.
actions: Sleep consistency protocol (same wake time daily)
Daily walk or aerobic exercise (BDNF stimulation)
Consider L-theanine 200 mg or ashwagandha 300–600 mg under supervision

impact: Cognitive performance and mood regulation are functioning but less than optimal.
actions: Reduce social media and screen time before bed
expected_outcome: Brain score reaching 65 achievable within 4–6 weeks.

impact: Tissue repair, hormonal balance and cellular regeneration are critically impaired.
actions: Clinician review for hormonal panel (cortisol, testosterone, thyroid)

impact: Repair, adaptation and hormonal recovery pathways are below threshold, slowing training adaptation and increasing injury risk.
